From the given information about the development of the cartilages of the larynx, we need to identify the embryonic origin. While none of the text explicitly mentions the exact source of laryngeal cartilage formation, understanding that the larynx develops during early fetal life provides context. Typically, branchial arches contribute significantly to the skeletal elements forming parts of the face and neck. Specifically, the fourth and sixth branchial arches play crucial roles in developing components of the larynx and thyroid gland. \boxed{D} Based on the evidence pointing towards significant contributions from both the fourth and sixth branchial arches to the development of laryngeal structures, I conclude that the correct answer must include both these arches.
